---
name: nezam-gh-security-compliance
description: Security and compliance workflow for GitHub repositories covering secret scanning, dependency audits, code scanning, and policy enforcement.

---
# Purpose
Automated security auditing and compliance hardening for pull requests and release branches with `/SCAN security` and `/FIX security` integration.

# Inputs
- `.github/` configuration files (workflows, CODEOWNERS, security policies).
- Dependency manifests and lockfiles.
- Existing security alerts and scan outputs.
- Repository access and branch protection settings.

# Step-by-Step Workflow
1. Inspect existing security controls:
   - Dependabot configuration and update cadence.
   - CodeQL setup and query packs.
   - Secret scanning and push protection status.
2. Ensure security baseline files exist and are current:
   - `SECURITY.md`
   - Dependabot config (`.github/dependabot.yml`)
   - Code scanning workflow (`.github/workflows/codeql.yml` or equivalent)
3. Enforce branch protection requirements for security-sensitive branches:
   - Required security checks.
   - Required reviews and dismissal controls.
4. Validate workflow permissions and OIDC/secrets usage for least privilege.
5. Run `/SCAN security` and classify findings by exploitability.
6. Apply `/FIX security` patch set:
   - Dependency upgrades.
   - Secret revocation/rotation actions.
   - Workflow policy hardening.
7. Re-run security scans and confirm closure of critical/high findings.
8. Record compliance results and deferred risks with owners.

# Validation & Metrics
- Critical/high vulnerability count after fixes (target: 0 open in active scope).
- Secret scanning detection/closure SLA.
- Security check enforcement on PRs (target: 100% required).
- Dependency freshness score and mean update lag.

# Output Format
- Security configuration files and workflow updates.
- Severity-ranked finding report with remediation status.
- Compliance checklist (Dependabot, CodeQL, secret scanning, branch protection).
- Deferred-risk register with owner and due date.

# Integration Hooks
- `/SCAN security` as primary trigger.
- `/FIX security` for deterministic remediation loop.
- `/SAVE log` for compliance evidence and risk acceptance record.
- CI/CD integration through required CodeQL and security check jobs.

# Anti-Patterns
- Hardcoded secrets in source or workflow YAML.
- Ignored security alerts without documented risk acceptance.
- Broad workflow permissions without justification.
- Security tooling enabled but not set as required checks.

# External Reference
- [GitHub Security overview](https://docs.github.com/code-security/getting-started/github-security-features)
- [CodeQL documentation](https://docs.github.com/code-security/code-scanning/automatically-scanning-your-code-for-vulnerabilities-and-errors)
- [Dependabot documentation](https://docs.github.com/code-security/dependabot)
- [Secret scanning documentation](https://docs.github.com/code-security/secret-scanning/introduction/about-secret-scanning)
